event_blocks.summary: Ciara O'Doherty, dressed in a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, with her dark hair pulled back, addresses the camera from a bright, minimalist setting against a white wardrobe. She begins by discussing the biggest trend stories for Spring Summer 2025, noting her extensive research to provide a concise list of trends, colors, and fabrics. An upbeat sound effect accompanies a title card displaying 'CIARA O'DOHERTY FASHION & LIFESTYLE' over a collage of her outfits, before returning to Ciara, who introduces herself and explains her channel's focus on high street basics, capsule wardrobes, and trend videos. As she encourages subscriptions, a YouTube-style 'SUBSCRIBE' button appears in the lower-left corner, which she gestures towards. Ciara then enthusiastically reveals suede as the dominating high street fabric, stating it's prevalent in handbags, footwear, and clothing. The screen transitions to a split-screen format, showing Ciara on the right while the left side displays images of women carrying various suede handbags in diverse outfits and settings, highlighting their versatility. She explains that suede, a heavier fabric with a nice texture, is prominent for spring summer in neutral colors like brown and mocha, as well as lighter shades such as beige, creams, baby blues, and pastels. A title card with 'SUEDE HANDBAGS' appears over a collage of these images. Ciara suggests accessories as an easy way to incorporate suede into an existing wardrobe. She then introduces a "new style" brown suede handbag, "The Gabrielle East West shoulder bag by the brand Perriza Wang," which she believes "hits so many different trend boxes." She praises mid-range brands like Perriza Wang for their "amazing quality suede and leather handbags for a fraction of the price of designer labels." Visuals transition to other brown suede handbags with gold clasps, shown on models in different outfits, emphasizing versatility. Ciara notes that Perriza Wang's core collection features classic, all-season styles that align with spring summer trends and transition well into autumn winter, praising their "really, really good quality suede fabrics." She also mentions owning and adoring "the Grace soft top handle bag in the suede," which she has been wearing "nonstop."

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She introduces "The Gabrielle East West shoulder bag by the brand Perriza Wang," praises the brand for its "amazing quality suede and leather handbags" at an affordable price, and mentions owning "the Grace soft top handle bag in the suede" which she "adores" and has been "wearing nonstop." Her voice is clear and enthusiastic.

Title Card.description: A black background with white text reading 'SUEDE HANDBAGS' overlaid on a collage of images showing women carrying various suede handbags in different outfits and settings.

Subscribe Button.description: A YouTube-style overlay in the lower-left corner with a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button, a bell icon, and the text 'CIARA O'DOHERTY Fashion & Style' next to a circular profile picture.

Suede Handbag Collage.description: A dynamic montage of images showing multiple women in diverse outfits carrying tan and olive suede handbags in various styles and settings. These are identified as "Perriza Wang" bags, including "The Gabrielle East West shoulder bag" and potentially "the Grace soft top handle bag," showcasing their "amazing quality suede and leather" and versatility for "spring summer" and "autumn winter" trends.

raw_segments: The video opens with Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, standing in a bright, minimalist setting against a white wardrobe with gold handles, a beige ceramic vase visible to her left. She speaks directly to the camera with an engaging, conversational tone, using expressive hand gestures as she begins discussing the biggest trend stories for Spring Summer 2025. Midway through, an upbeat sound effect accompanies a title card displaying 'CIARA O'DOHERTY FASHION & LIFESTYLE' over a collage of her stylish outfits. The video then returns to Ciara, who warmly greets viewers, introduces herself as Kira, and explains that her channel focuses on shopping for high street basics, building capsule wardrobes, and fun trend videos. As she encourages viewers to subscribe, a YouTube-style 'SUBSCRIBE' button with her channel name 'CIARA O'DOHERTY Fashion & Style' and a bell icon appears in the lower-left corner, which she gestures towards. She elaborates that today's video will cover the biggest fashion trends and main aesthetics for Spring and Summer 2025, noting her extensive research to provide a concise list of trends, colors, and fabrics, before transitioning to discuss the biggest fabric trend.

The video opens with Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase. She enthusiastically asks for guesses on the dominating high street fabric, immediately revealing it to be suede, stating that "suede fabrics are everywhere across our handbags, our footwear, in our clothing." The screen then transitions to a split-screen format, with Ciara on the right continuing to speak and gesture, while the left side dynamically displays a sequence of fashion images. These images showcase various women carrying different styles of tan and olive suede handbags in diverse outfits and settings—ranging from streetwear to more formal ensembles, highlighting the bags' versatility as shoulder bags, crossbody bags, and even with decorative accessories. Ciara explains that suede, a heavier fabric with a nice texture, was prominent in autumn/winter and is now seen "so much for spring summer as well." She notes that suede is appearing not only in key neutral colors like brown and mocha but also in lighter shades such as beige, creams, baby blues, and pastels. As she continues to speak with enthusiasm, maintaining eye contact and using hand gestures, a title card appears in the top left corner with the text 'SUEDE HANDBAGS' over a collage of the previously shown images, reinforcing the theme. Ciara encourages viewers who have been tempted to pick up something in suede, suggesting accessories as an easy way to style it with an existing wardrobe, before the audio cuts off as she mentions having an entire video dedicated to her favorites.

Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She introduces a brown suede handbag, holding it up, and enthusiastically states she has a "new style" she thinks viewers will "absolutely love" because "it hits so many different trend boxes." She identifies it as "The Gabrielle East West shoulder bag by the brand Perriza Wang." As she continues to narrate, the screen splits: on the right, Ciara gestures while holding the bag, maintaining eye contact, while on the left, a dynamic collage of images appears. This collage showcases different women modeling similar suede handbags in various outfits and settings, including a model in a white coat and another in a striped shirt and wide-leg pants. Ciara expresses her admiration for "mid-range and kind of mid-luxury bag brands," highlighting Perriza Wang for their "most amazing quality suede and leather handbags for a fraction of the price of designer labels." The visual sequence transitions to a different brown suede handbag with a gold clasp, shown on a woman in a green jacket and then on a model in a white turtleneck, emphasizing the bags’ versatility. Ciara explains that Perriza Wang has a "core collection of really simple but classic styles" that are "all season round," hitting "so many spring summer trend notes" but also working "so well coming into autumn winter." She praises their "really, really good quality suede fabrics" and mentions another bag she owns and "adores," "the Grace soft top handle bag in the suede," which she picked up "a few months ago" and has been "wearing it nonstop." Ciara continues to narrate and gesture throughout, while the split-screen visuals highlight the handbags’ styling potential across different looks and environments.

event_blocks.summary: Ciara O'Doherty, dressed in a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, presented a structured brown suede handbag, identifying it as the "Madison top handle bag." She described the bag as having a "really, really classic shape," available in "three different sizes," and now offered in "suede fabric." Ciara highlighted its "chocolatey" yet "slightly warmer chocolate" color, suitable for summer, and noted its "East-West shape" trend, seen on runways from Miu Miu and Prada. She demonstrated the bag's functionality, opening it to reveal a main interior chamber capable of fitting a "smaller size tablet" and "any sized phone," along with an "interior little flat pocket" and "two separate pockets on either side." Ciara also pointed out the "gorgeous long top handle" and the "feet on the bag" for material protection. A split-screen simultaneously showed a model posing with the bag and a product image of the bag filled with items. Ciara mentioned that her discount code, 'CIARA12', for 12% off Periza Wine pieces, had been reactivated. She emphasized the bag's versatility, stating it would "look beautiful with lighter colors, with denims," and called it a "really, really easy shade" she gets "so much wear out of." She also advised that suede is a "very, very good" trend to invest in, as it's "going absolutely nowhere," and noted how the bag complements her "very classic and very minimal" outfits by adding "a little pop of something with my accessories."

Suede Handbag Collage.description: A dynamic montage of images showing multiple women in diverse outfits carrying tan and olive suede handbags in various styles and settings, including street scenes, cafes, and indoor environments, highlighting the bags' versatility and textures.

Brown Suede Handbag.description: A structured, medium-sized brown suede handbag with dual top handles, gold hardware, and a soft, textured finish. Ciara O'Doherty showcases it, describing it as an 'easy shade' that looks 'beautiful with lighter colors, with denims.' She highlights its spacious 'main interior chamber' that fits a 'smaller size tablet' and 'any sized phone,' along with an 'interior little flat pocket' and 'two separate pockets on either side.' It features a 'gorgeous long top handle' in an 'East-West style' and 'feet on the bag' for protection. She presents it as a versatile accessory for 'classic and very minimal' outfits, suggesting it adds a 'pop of something' and is a good investment as 'suede as a trend' is enduring.

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase in the background. She has a clear, engaging voice, explaining the handbag's features, discussing styling options, and advocating for suede as a lasting trend.

She gestures with her hands as she introduces what she calls the "Madison top handle bag," holding up a structured brown suede handbag to the camera. She further describes the bag's color as a "chocolatey" but "slightly warmer chocolate," ideal for summer. Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, presents a structured brown suede handbag in a minimalist indoor setting. Speaking in a clear, engaging voice, she immediately describes how the bag "would look beautiful with lighter colors, with denims," calling it a "really, really easy shade" and noting she gets "so much wear out of them." She holds the bag's dual top handles, showcasing its soft, textured finish and gold hardware. She then opens the bag to reveal its interior, explaining, "you have your main interior chamber here. It actually fits quite a lot," including a "smaller size tablet" and "any sized phone," emphasizing it has "plenty of room." A split-screen comparison simultaneously displays a product image of the bag filled with items. She gestures expressively, highlighting an "interior little flat pocket" and "two separate pockets on either side." She points out the "gorgeous long top handle in the very much the East-West style" and mentions the "feet on the bag," which she appreciates for protecting the suede material. Maintaining eye contact, she discusses styling, stating that "if you're someone who's interested in suede as a trend, I do think it is a very, very good one to invest in," as it's "going absolutely nowhere." She concludes by noting how it complements her "very classic and very minimal" outfits, adding "a little pop of something with my accessories." The bright, even lighting consistently highlights the bag’s rich brown suede and the clean, modern aesthetic.

event_blocks.summary: Ciara O'Doherty, dressed in a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, spoke expressively from a minimalist indoor setting about fashion trends and styling tips. Initially, she discussed the "gorgeous bit of texture" certain items add, encouraging viewer engagement through links and discount codes, and expressing her fondness for seeing them style "beautiful bags." She then introduced Spring/Summer 2025 color trends, beginning with Pantone's 'Mocha Mousse,' described as "the ultimate neutral," a "very creamy, kind of a lighter brown color," and "such an easy one to style." This was visually supported by a split-screen collage showcasing the color in various applications, from chocolate and fashion to accessories and models in brown and beige outfits, with Ciara noting brown's significant presence in 2024 and its effectiveness in offsetting lighter hues. Transitioning, she highlighted 'Butter Yellow' as another major trend, describing it as a creamy, warm-toned, almost vintage-inspired yellow seen across clothing, accessories, and homeware, further illustrated by street-style images of women in butter-yellow ensembles. Ciara then introduced 'Candy Pink,' acknowledging it as "huge this spring/summer" and a "very feminine, floaty" color, despite her personal preference for blue, emphasizing its ease of styling with neutrals or accessories like neckerchiefs, with visuals showing diverse candy-pink outfits. Finally, she discussed 'Chartreuse Green,' a "very specific shade of green," a "vintage style color" that gives a "nod to the mid-century," presenting it as "huge for spring summer" through collages and street-style images of women in chartreuse outfits, including a green coat with a fur collar.

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase in the background. She speaks with a clear, engaging voice, discussing fashion trends, asking viewers about their preferences for 'candy pink,' and introducing 'chartreuse' as a 'very specific shade of green' and a 'vintage style color' that's 'huge for spring summer.'

Pantone Mocha Mousse Collage.description: A dynamic split-screen collage on the left side of the frame showcasing the color 'Mocha Mousse' (PANTONE 17-1230 TCX) in various contexts, including fashion and accessories, featuring models in coordinated brown and beige outfits. Ciara's audio identifies this as a significant trend for 2025, highlighting brown's versatility in offsetting lighter colors.

2025 COLOUR TRENDS: BUTTER YELLOW Collag.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying soft yellow and cream fashion items, with two models wearing coordinated butter-yellow outfits. Ciara's audio introduces this as a major Spring/Summer 2025 trend, describing it as a creamy, warm-toned, almost vintage-inspired yellow seen across various categories.

Butter Yellow Street Style Montage.description: A dynamic sequence of real-life street-style images on the left side of the frame showing women in butter-yellow outfits. Ciara's audio accompanies this, discussing the styling of butter yellow and its widespread presence.

Candy Pink Street Style Montage.description: A dynamic sequence of real-life street-style images on the left side of the frame showing women in candy-pink outfits—such as a pink turtleneck under a beige coat, a pink off-the-shoulder top with wide-leg trousers, a pink sweater with dark pants, a pink turtleneck under a brown leather jacket, and a white blazer with pink corduroy pants—highlighting the color's versatility in everyday wear, as Ciara O'Doherty discusses 'candy pink' as a color to 'tiptoe into' with accessories.

2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CANDY PINK Collage.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying soft pink fashion items including sweaters, trousers, dresses, and accessories, with two models wearing coordinated candy-pink outfits, and text reading '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CANDY PINK' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'. This visual directly illustrates Ciara's spoken introduction of candy pink as a significant trend for spring/summer.

Chartreuse Green Runway Look.description: A model in a vibrant green dress with a high slit, laughing on a runway, showcasing the boldness and energy of the chartreuse green trend, as Ciara O'Doherty discusses the color's 'vintage style' and mid-century nod.

Chartreuse Green Street Style Montage.description: A dynamic sequence of real-life street-style images on the left side of the frame showing women in chartreuse green outfits—such as a woman in a green sweater with red shoes, a woman in a green skirt with orange heels, and a woman in a green top with a white blazer—highlighting the color's versatility in everyday wear, while Ciara O'Doherty describes chartreuse as a 'vintage style color' that gives a 'nod to the mid-century.'

2025 TRENDS BABY PINK Collage.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying soft pink fashion items including sweaters, trousers, dresses, and accessories, with two models wearing coordinated candy-pink outfits, and text reading '2025 TRENDS BABY PINK' and '@CIARAODOHERTY', appearing as Ciara O'Doherty suggests using accessories to introduce oneself to a new color like candy pink.

Green Coat with Fur Collar.description: A close-up of a green coat with a textured fur collar, emphasizing the luxurious and dramatic elements of the chartreuse green fashion trend, appearing as Ciara O'Doherty elaborates on the 'vintage style' and mid-century influences of chartreuse.

2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CHARTREUSE GREEN Col.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying chartreuse green fashion items including t-shirts, tops, dresses, and accessories, with two models wearing coordinated green outfits, and text reading '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CHARTREUSE GREEN' and '@CIARAODOHERTY', introduced by Ciara O'Doherty as a color that is 'huge for spring summer' and a 'very specific shade of green' with a 'vintage style' feel.

raw_segments: The video opens with Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She discusses how certain items add a "gorgeous bit of texture" to an outfit, encouraging viewers to check links, use her discount code, and share their purchases, expressing her love for seeing them style "beautiful bags." Transitioning, she introduces color trends for Spring/Summer 2025, specifically highlighting 'Mocha Mousse' by Pantone. As she speaks, a split-screen appears to her left, displaying a dynamic collage of images labeled 'PANTONE 17-1230 TCX Mocha Mousse', showcasing the color in chocolate, fashion, and accessories, including models in brown and beige outfits, street-style, and runway looks. Ciara explains that Mocha Mousse was Pantone's color of the year for 2025, describing it as "the ultimate neutral," a "very creamy, kind of a lighter brown color," and "such an easy one to style" for both spring/summer and autumn. The collage continues to evolve, showing various applications in clothing, footwear, and handbags, including a close-up of brown suede boots, while Ciara also mentions including "brown earthy tones" as brown had a significant moment in 2024. The bright, even lighting maintains a professional and stylish atmosphere focused on fashion and color trends.

The video opens with a split-screen, featuring Ciara O'Doherty on the right, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings. She discusses brown as one of the biggest colors for 2025, noting its presence in spring/summer despite being a darker wintery color, and its effectiveness in offsetting lighter hues, suggesting it's a clever, easy color. On the left, a fashion runway sequence showcases models in flowing, ruffled mocha-toned outfits, including sheer dresses and matching boots. This transitions to a static collage titled '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: MOCHA MOUSSE' by @CIARAODOHERTY, displaying coordinated brown and beige garments. Ciara mentions she will have dedicated shopping edits for these trends on her LTK page. The left side then shifts to a new collage titled '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: BUTTER YELLOW', featuring soft yellow and cream outfits, as Ciara introduces butter yellow as another major trend for Spring/Summer 2025, describing it as a creamy, warm-toned, almost vintage-inspired yellow seen across clothing, accessories, and even homeware. This is followed by real-life street-style images of women wearing butter-yellow ensembles, such as a long yellow dress with a croissant, a yellow cardigan with striped shorts, and a yellow knit dress with a brown jacket, while Ciara continues to speak animatedly about styling the color. The bright, even lighting throughout emphasizes the colors and textures, maintaining an informative and stylish atmosphere focused on upcoming fashion color trends.

The video opens with Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She advises that styling bright colors isn't as difficult as it might seem, suggesting pairing them with neutrals like creams, beiges, or a pop of brown. She mentions her recent acquisition of a beautiful butter-yellow two-piece suit from COS, which aligns with the dynamic montage on the left side of the split-screen showcasing various women in stylish outfits, including a woman in a cream-colored suit, a dark coat over a yellow dress, and a butter-yellow suit. Ciara then transitions to discussing "candy pink" as another huge color for spring/summer. As she speaks, the left side of the screen displays a static collage titled '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CANDY PINK', featuring an array of pink garments and accessories, with her handle '@CIARAODOHERTY' at the bottom. She admits she personally tends to shy away from pink, often preferring blue for feminine, floaty looks, but emphasizes that candy pink is "huge this spring/summer" and will be seen much more on the high street. This commentary is visually reinforced by a dynamic sequence of real-life street-style images on the left, showing women in diverse candy-pink outfits—from a pink turtleneck under a beige coat to a pink off-the-shoulder top with wide-leg trousers, and a white blazer with pink corduroy pants—demonstrating its versatility and ease of styling across most skin tones and hair colors.

Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures from a minimalist indoor setting. She asks viewers if "candy pink" is a color they like or have in their wardrobe, suggesting it's an easy way to "tiptoe into a color" by using accessories like a neckerchief or scarf. As she speaks, the left side of the split-screen dynamically showcases 2025 fashion trends, beginning with a close-up of a pink satin dress, transitioning to a model in a full pink ensemble on a runway, then a woman in a pink dress on a couch, followed by a woman in a white blazer and pink corduroy pants, a model in a sleeveless pink top and skirt with a black clutch, and finally a woman in a pink cardigan and burgundy trousers. A collage titled '2025 TRENDS BABY PINK' then appears, displaying various pink garments and accessories. Ciara then introduces "chartreuse," admitting it's a color she doesn't wear often but notes it's "huge for spring summer" and describes it as a "very specific shade of green," almost like a "vintage style color" that gives a "nod to the mid-century." Concurrently, the visuals shift to a collage titled '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CHARTREUSE GREEN', featuring various green fashion items and models. This is followed by street-style images of women in chartreuse green outfits, including a woman in a green sweater with red shoes and another in a green skirt with orange heels. The segment concludes with a model in a vibrant green dress with a high slit, laughing on a runway, and a close-up of a green coat with a fur collar, all while Ciara continues her commentary.

event_blocks.summary: Ciara O'Doherty, dressed in a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, spoke from a minimalist indoor setting, explaining that earthier tones are easier to style than neons, with the high street offering good options. As she discussed the 'Chartreuse Green' trend, a montage on the left displayed various high-fashion and street-style looks in the vibrant color, including models in lime green coats, olive green coats with chartreuse scarves, flowing chartreuse dresses, hooded chartreuse coats, chartreuse slip dresses, ruffled chartreuse dresses, draped chartreuse satin dresses, and chartreuse wrap dresses with fringe. Ciara noted Chartreuse's suitability for occasion and evening wear, though she personally preferred olive green for day-to-day, and then asked the audience for their stance on the color. The visual shifted to a static collage for the 'Tangerine Dream' trend, featuring orange fashion items and the text '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: TANGERINE DREAM' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'. A runway model appeared in an off-the-shoulder tangerine gown, with a Pantone swatch labeled 'PANTONE 15-1335 Tangelo'. Ciara introduced Tangerine as the final Spring/Summer 2025 color, describing it as a 'punchy,' 'zesty' orange, not quite neon, ideal for the high summer season. She enthusiastically described tangerine as a 'gorgeous summery color,' perfect for holidays and warm evenings, as the montage showed a woman in an orange oversized shirt and shorts, an orange shirt with jeans, and an orange shirt with jeans and orange heels. Ciara highlighted the color's presence on the high street and its suitability for occasion wear, like wedding guest dresses, with visuals showing a woman in a long orange dress with a green handbag, a ruffled orange dress, and an orange wrap dress with blue heels, noting its beauty for full-length summer or spring wedding dresses. The montage briefly included a red lace dress and a yellow shirt with an orange scarf before returning to orange outfits, including one with a red bandana and a flowing one-shoulder orange dress. Ciara advised incorporating this 'punchy tangerine, almost airing into like a coral red' into accessories like neckerchiefs, bangles, earrings, or lipstick, concluding with a close-up of an orange satin shirt and a matching crocodile-textured handbag.

Chartreuse Green Runway Look.description: Models on a runway showcasing the Chartreuse Green trend, including one in a long olive green coat with a chartreuse scarf and sunglasses, and another in a long chartreuse dress with a hooded coat, complementing Ciara O'Doherty's discussion of the color's suitability for evening and occasion wear.

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase in the background. She speaks clearly and enthusiastically about the 'TANGERINE DREAM' color trend, describing it as a 'gorgeous summery color' perfect for holidays and occasion wear, and suggesting its incorporation into accessories, noting its 'punchy' quality that verges on 'coral red'.

Chartreuse Green Street Style Montage.description: A dynamic sequence of real-life street-style images on the left side of the frame showing women in chartreuse green outfits—such as a woman in a green sweater with red shoes, a woman in a green skirt with orange heels, and a woman in a green top with a white blazer—highlighting the color's versatility in everyday wear, visually illustrating the Chartreuse Green trend as Ciara O'Doherty discusses its styling versatility for occasion and evening wear.

Tangerine Runway Look.description: A model on a runway wearing an off-the-shoulder tangerine gown, with a Pantone color swatch labeled 'PANTONE 15-1335 Tangelo' in the top left corner, visually representing the 'zesty' and beautiful Tangerine trend as described by Ciara O'Doherty.

2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CHARTREUSE GREEN Col.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying chartreuse green fashion items including t-shirts, tops, dresses, and accessories, with two models wearing coordinated green outfits, and text reading '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: CHARTREUSE GREEN' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'.

2025 COLOUR TRENDS: TANGERINE DREAM Coll.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying orange fashion items including a tank top, dress, handbag, sneakers, cardigan, and sandals, with two models wearing coordinated orange outfits, and text reading '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: TANGERINE DREAM' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'.

Orange Outfit with Red Bandana.description: A woman in an orange shirt and shorts with a red bandana on her head, standing in front of a lush green bush with orange flowers, wearing sunglasses.

Orange Shirt with Jeans and Heels.description: A woman in an orange shirt and light blue jeans, wearing orange heels, standing indoors with a coffee table and a book titled 'THE BOOK' visible.

Orange Dress with Green Bag.description: A woman in a long orange dress with a green handbag, standing on a city sidewalk with parked cars and a scooter in the background.

One-Shoulder Orange Dress.description: A woman in a flowing one-shoulder orange dress, walking on a sandy path with a weathered wall and tropical plants in the background.

Orange Shirt and Shorts Outfit.description: A woman wearing an orange oversized shirt and matching shorts over a crochet bra, taking a mirror selfie in a room with wooden flooring and a patterned chair.

Orange Shirt with Jeans Outfit.description: A woman in an orange shirt and light blue jeans, posing in a bathroom with a green phone case, showcasing a casual street-style look.

Orange Wrap Dress with Blue Heels.description: A woman in an orange wrap dress with a high slit, wearing bright blue heels and holding a white clutch, posing indoors against a white paneled wall.

Red Lace Dress.description: A woman in a red lace off-the-shoulder dress, holding a black handbag, smiling in a modern indoor lounge with large windows and contemporary furniture.

Ruffled Orange Dress.description: A woman in a ruffled orange dress with a striped bag, standing on a tiled floor, adjusting the dress’s tie detail.

Orange Satin Shirt with Crocodile Bag.description: A close-up of a woman wearing an orange satin shirt with a matching crocodile-textured handbag, highlighting the luxurious texture and color coordination.

Yellow Shirt with Orange Scarf.description: A woman in a bright yellow shirt with an orange patterned scarf tied around her neck, smiling in a well-lit indoor setting.

raw_segments: Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures from a minimalist indoor setting. She begins by explaining that rather muted, slightly earthier tones are easier to style than neons and bright shades, and that the high street offers good options. As she discusses the 'Chartreuse Green' trend, a dynamic montage on the left side of the split-screen showcases various high-fashion and street-style looks in the vibrant color. This sequence includes a model in a lime green coat, another in a long olive green coat with a chartreuse scarf on a runway, a woman in a flowing chartreuse dress, a model in a long chartreuse dress with a hooded coat, a woman in a chartreuse slip dress, a woman in a chartreuse ruffled dress stepping from a limousine, a model in a draped chartreuse satin dress, and another in a chartreuse wrap dress with fringe details. Ciara elaborates that Chartreuse can be styled beautifully, especially for occasion and evening wear, though she personally would gravitate towards an olive green for day-to-day. She then engages the audience, asking where they stand on Chartreuse. The visual then shifts to a static collage displaying orange fashion items for the 'Tangerine Dream' trend, accompanied by text '2025 COLOUR TRENDS: TANGERINE DREAM' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'. This is followed by a model on a runway in an off-the-shoulder tangerine gown, with a Pantone color swatch labeled 'PANTONE 15-1335 Tangelo'. Ciara introduces Tangerine as the last color for Spring/Summer 2025, describing it as a 'punchy orange color' that's not quite neon, but a 'zesty' and beautiful shade to style for the high summer season.

The video opens with a split-screen, featuring Ciara O'Doherty on the right, speaking expressively with hand gestures while wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings against a minimalist backdrop. On the left, a dynamic montage showcases the 'TANGERINE DREAM' color trend. Ciara enthusiastically describes tangerine as a 'gorgeous summery color,' ideal for holidays, evoking sunsets and warm evenings, as the montage begins with a woman in an orange oversized shirt and shorts over a crochet bra, followed by a close-up of an orange shirt, and then a woman in an orange shirt with jeans. Ciara notes the color's increasing presence on the high street and its suitability for 'occasion wear,' such as a 'wedding guest dress,' while the visuals transition to a woman in a long orange dress with a green handbag, then an orange shirt with jeans and orange heels, a ruffled orange dress, and an orange wrap dress with blue heels. She further suggests it's beautiful for full-length dresses for summer or spring weddings. The montage briefly includes a woman in a red lace dress and another in a yellow shirt with an orange scarf, before returning to orange with a woman in an orange outfit and red bandana, and a flowing one-shoulder orange dress. Ciara advises incorporating this 'punchy tangerine, almost airing into like a coral red' into accessories like neckerchiefs, bangles, earrings, or even lipstick, as the segment concludes with a luxurious close-up of an orange satin shirt paired with a matching crocodile-textured handbag.

event_blocks.summary: Ciara O'Doherty, presenting from a minimalist indoor setting, introduced Spring Summer fashion trends categorized into three groups, beginning with Elevated Boho. She noted the resurgence of Boho, a trend absent for several years, with brands like Chloé embodying the style through ruffles, belts, and tassels, exemplified by runway looks featuring mustard ruffled dresses and street style images of women in ruffled tops and jumpsuits. Ciara discussed how this trend translates to the high street with pieces like oversized ruffled blouses, showcasing models in white lace dresses with belts and pink ruffled blouses with jeans, and highlighting "And Other Stories" for their maximalist ruffles. The presentation also touched upon a "polished boho" collage featuring cream and beige ensembles with lace and ruffles, and a final runway look with a mustard suede jacket and ruffled skirt. Transitioning to "Western vibes," Ciara emphasized denim's timeless appeal and the growing trend of Western denim, including double denim and Western shirts, drawing inspiration from her experiences in Austin and plans for Nashville. She advocated for subtle "nods" to the trend, citing Sezane as a brand excelling in wearable Western-inspired pieces, with visuals displaying cow-print jackets, fringe, and cowboy boots. Further elaborating on Western and Boho influences, she highlighted "cow print and snake skin" as significant trends, favoring their neutral tones and versatility, with visuals of cow-print jackets, studded belts, and cowboy boots. The discussion also encompassed "vintage nods," specifically 80s and maximalist styles, featuring "oversized earrings," "peplum styles," "nipped-in waists," and "power shoulders" seen in structured blazers and suits, embodying a "power businesswoman aesthetic." Micro-trends like "pillbox hats" and "structured round hats" were also presented as "very, very fun" for occasion wear, with Capri trousers mentioned as an emerging trend.

Runway Look - Mustard Ruffled Dress.description: A model on a runway wearing a mustard-colored ruffled dress with a high neck, layered ruffles, and sunglasses, holding a black clutch, exemplifying the 'ruffles' and 'floaty fabric' elements of the bohemian trend discussed by Ciara.

Cream and Beige Ensemble Collage.description: A collage of three models in cream and beige outfits featuring lace, ruffles, and structured jackets, with one model wearing a white utility jacket, another in a yellow jacket with a lace skirt, and the third in a long cream coat with a lace top.

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase in the background, discussing fashion trends like 'vintage nods,' '80s maximalism,' 'power shoulders,' 'structured hats,' and 'Capri trousers' with an engaging and informative tone.

Street Style - Brown Leather Jacket with.description: A woman in a brown leather jacket over a white lace crop top and denim shorts, accessorized with a brown handbag and layered necklaces, walking on a city sidewalk.

Street Style - Black Jumpsuit with State.description: A woman in a black jumpsuit with a wide belt and a long, ornate necklace, wearing sunglasses, walking confidently on a city street.

Woman in Pink Ruffled Blouse with Jeans.description: A woman wearing a pink ruffled blouse with drawstring sleeves and jeans, accessorized with large sunglasses and layered necklaces, posing against a white background.

Street Style - Grey Ruffled Top with Sca.description: A woman walking on a city street wearing a grey ruffled top, a long scarf, and brown boots, holding a coffee cup, embodying a casual boho look that aligns with Ciara's mention of 'ruffles' as a key bohemian trend element.

Model in White Lace Dress with Brown Bel.description: A model on a runway wearing a white lace dress with a brown belt, long sleeves, and a high neckline, showcasing a refined boho look that incorporates 'sheer' elements and a prominent 'belt,' as highlighted in Ciara's commentary.

Runway Look - Mustard Suede Jacket with.description: A model on a reflective runway wearing a mustard suede jacket over a blue top and a long, ruffled brown skirt, accessorized with a beaded necklace and sunglasses, embodying a bold boho-chic look that perfectly illustrates Ciara's points about 'suede' fabrics and 'ruffles' tying into both bohemian and Western themes.

2025 TRENDS POLISHED BOHO Collage.description: A static collage on the left side of the frame displaying denim and floral fashion items including a denim vest, flared jeans, ruffled skirts, and a white blouse, with two models wearing coordinated boho outfits, and text reading '2025 TRENDS POLISHED BOHO' and '@CIARAODOHERTY'.

2025 TRENDS DENIM TRENDS Collage.description: A dynamic collage on the left side of the frame, illustrating the '2025 TRENDS DENIM TRENDS' with various denim garments and styling, including vests, pants, jackets, and a cap, with models showcasing layered looks, suede jackets, and cowboy boots. This visual content directly complements Ciara's commentary on 'Western denim,' 'double denim,' 'Western shirts,' and 'mixing and matching different shades of denim,' emphasizing wearable and cool styling options.

Western Fashion Montage.description: A dynamic sequence of images on the left side of the frame showcasing Western and bohemian-inspired fashion, including models and street-style individuals in cowboy hats, fringe jackets, cow-print garments, plaid shirts, and denim ensembles, with accessories like belts, handbags, and boots. These visuals directly illustrate the specific fashion trends and styling elements, such as cow print, snake skin, and cowboy boots, being discussed by Ciara O'Doherty in her commentary.

raw_segments: Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She begins by stating, "Now we're going to move on to Spring Summer fashion trends," explaining she's grouped them into "three different categories, starting with Elevated Boho." As she speaks, a static collage titled '2025 TRENDS POLISHED BOHO' by @CIARAODOHERTY appears on the left, displaying denim and floral pieces, including a denim vest, flared jeans, and ruffled skirts. Ciara elaborates that she "touched on this in the New Year as being a huge trend for 2025 in general," noting, "It's not just for Spring Summer, we're going to be seeing it in Autumn Winter as well," highlighting "this revival of Boho, which we really didn't see for the last few years." She mentions, "Brands like Chloé really nailed this, the typical Chloé girl being covered in oversized ruffles and belts and tassels and chunky kind of wooden platform wedges," adding, "Like it's very, very cool." During this, a runway image of a model in a mustard ruffled dress with a high neck and sunglasses is shown, followed by street-style images: a woman in a grey ruffled top with a scarf, another in a brown leather jacket over a lace top, and a third in a black jumpsuit with a statement necklace. Ciara then asks, "Looking at it in a more wearable sense, how is this filtering into the high street?" and suggests "Things like an oversized ruffled blouse." A model in a white lace dress with a brown belt is displayed, then a woman in a pink ruffled blouse with jeans. Ciara emphasizes, "This is a key piece that encapsulates the whole trend, but that you can style in lots of different ways," and credits "And Other Stories I feel like have nailed the oversized kind of maximalist ruffles." A collage of models in cream and beige ensembles with lace and ruffles appears, followed by a final image of a model in a mustard suede jacket over a blue top and a long ruffled skirt on a reflective runway. Ciara concludes by advising, "My top pick would be to go for an oversized ruffled blouse and then you can style it with your denim jeans, you can wear it with skirts, you could do maxi skirts, you could..." as she continues to gesture animatedly, maintaining eye contact with the camera.

Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures from a minimalist indoor setting, providing clear commentary on current fashion trends. On the left side of the split-screen, a dynamic montage of fashion images illustrates her points. Ciara explains that the best way to approach trends is to style key pieces like mini skirts, trousers, and shorts in many different ways, rather than overhauling a whole wardrobe. As she discusses the bohemian trend, highlighting elements like 'ruffles' and 'floaty fabrics,' the montage displays a woman in a mustard yellow ruffled dress walking on a city street, followed by another in a beige ruffled dress outdoors, and a model in a tan sheer dress with a gold chain belt and brown knee-high boots on a runway. Ciara continues, mentioning 'looser trapeze-style dresses' and 'elements of sheer,' while the visuals transition to a woman in a cream off-the-shoulder top and white skirt with a studded belt in a tropical setting, then a woman in a blue sleeveless crop top and black wide-leg pants with a studded belt near a metal staircase, and another in a cream ribbed top and matching pants with a black scalloped belt indoors. She then emphasizes 'oversized chunky belts,' 'double belts,' and 'belts layered onto each other,' noting how this 'touches on the Western style.' The montage continues with a woman in a beige oversized shirt and white lace skirt with brown knee-high boots outdoors, and a woman in a cow-print jacket, white crop top, and blue jeans with a brown belt, seen from the back. Ciara highlights that fabrics like 'suede' also tie into both the boho and Western themes, exemplified by a model on a runway wearing a mustard suede jacket over a blue top and a long, ruffled brown skirt. She concludes by stating that many of these trends 'meld into each other,' and it's about figuring out the 'key trend stories,' before transitioning to discuss 'Western vibes' as a very large trend.

The segment opens with Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures from a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase. As she speaks with a clear, engaging voice, a dynamic collage titled '2025 TRENDS DENIM TRENDS' unfolds on the left, showcasing various denim garments and styling, including vests, pants, jackets, and a cap, with models illustrating layered looks, suede jackets, and cowboy boots. Ciara immediately delves into the 'Western trend,' emphasizing that 'denim in general is always on trend' and a 'timeless classic,' but notes a strong move 'more and more into Western denim.' She details elements like 'double denim,' 'Western shirts buttoned up to the top,' and 'mixing and matching different shades of denim,' including 'darker, indigo denims,' all of which are visually represented in the evolving collage. She explains that this trend is building momentum across music and pop culture, not just fashion, and shares her personal 'love affair' with these styles after spending time in Austin and planning trips to Nashville and Kentucky. Ciara stresses that the trend is 'done in a very wearable way,' advocating for 'little nods' rather than a full costume, and praises brands like Sezane for doing 'Western nods really, really well' while still remaining 'really cool,' as the collage continues to display diverse and practical denim outfits.

The video features a split-screen, with Ciara O'Doherty on the right, speaking expressively and gesturing in a minimalist indoor setting with white cabinetry and a beige vase. She wears a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, maintaining a professional yet approachable demeanor. Ciara's clear and articulate voice guides the viewer through current fashion trends. On the left, a dynamic montage of fashion images cycles through various Western and bohemian-inspired looks, visually illustrating Ciara's commentary. She begins by discussing a French brand known for its 'slight bohemian, slight Western kind of vein,' highlighting their 'embroidery,' 'cut-out details,' and 'slightly Western shirts.' As she speaks, the montage showcases models and street-style individuals in cowboy hats, fringe jackets, cow-print garments, plaid shirts, and denim ensembles, including a paisley blouse, a brown fringed dress, and a cow-print jacket. Ciara then emphasizes 'cow print and snake skin' as 'really, really big trends' for spring/summer, expressing her personal preference for 'horse hair cow print' and 'snake skin print' due to their 'neutral colors' and ease of styling. The visuals on the left continue to display these prints and related accessories like belts and handbags, which Ciara suggests as great ways to incorporate the trends. She also mentions 'cowboy boots and Western boots in general,' noting their consistent popularity, while the montage features outfits with various cowboy boots. The bright, even lighting throughout both sides of the screen, from runway settings to urban streets and rustic barns on the left, complements Ciara's engaging discussion, creating a cohesive and informative presentation on Western-chic aesthetics.

Ciara O'Doherty, a woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively with hand gestures in a minimalist indoor setting. She discusses fashion trends, transitioning from "Western details" to "vintage nods," specifically highlighting "80s" and "maximalist" styles. As she speaks, a dynamic split-screen montage on the left showcases various fashion looks that exemplify her points. She mentions "oversized earrings" and "door knocker earrings," visually echoed by a model in a black leather jacket over a lime green ruffled top with large gold and black statement earrings. Ciara describes "peplum styles," "nipped-in waist," and the "power shoulder," aligning with the structured silhouettes seen in looks like a model in a black turtleneck and oversized blazer, a dramatic black suit with a wide-brimmed hat, and various women in blazers (beige, plaid, white, grey, black) often paired with skirts or pants, embodying the "power businesswoman aesthetic." She then shifts to "micro-trends" like the "pillbox hat" and "structured round hats," which are illustrated by models wearing a wide-brimmed hat, a black headpiece, and various black hats with different outfits. Ciara emphasizes these trends are "very, very fun, especially for occasion wear." She concludes by mentioning "Capri trousers" as a trend gaining popularity, though no specific visual example of Capri trousers is shown in the montage. Throughout, Ciara's expressive speech and gestures complement the visual display of structured silhouettes, bold accessories, and monochromatic elegance.

event_blocks.summary: Initially presented in a split-screen format, the left side displayed a vibrant montage of women in urban settings, exuding confidence in monochromatic street-style fashion, including black pinstripe blazers, one-shoulder tops, white trousers, sleek black suits, and designer handbags. Concurrently, on the right, Ciara O'Doherty, dressed in a blue denim shirt, oversized rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, addressed the camera from a minimalist indoor space featuring white cabinetry and a beige vase. Ciara spoke with expressive hand gestures about fashion trends, highlighting peep-toe shoes as a returning vintage-inspired trend, acknowledging its divisive nature but emphasizing its cyclical return in fashion. She then transitioned to a full-screen view, providing an overview of key color trends and aesthetic trends for 2025, specifically for spring and summer. Ciara actively engaged the audience by soliciting their opinions on these trends, asking about their preferences and hesitations. She prompted viewers to "give me a thumbs up" if they enjoyed the content and desired more trend-related videos, at which point a YouTube-style 'SUBSCRIBE' button overlay appeared in the lower-left corner, branded with 'CIARA O'DOHERTY' and 'Fashion & Style,' complete with a profile picture and bell icon. Ciara then directly encouraged viewers to "be sure to subscribe to my channel," noting her weekly video uploads and expressing her desire for their return, before concluding with a thank you for watching, all under bright, even lighting that maintained a professional aesthetic.

Ciara O'Doherty.description: A woman with dark hair pulled back, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a clear, engaging voice about fashion trends like peep-toe shoes, the cyclical nature of style, and upcoming 2025 color trends and spring/summer aesthetics, before encouraging viewers to like and subscribe to her channel.

Subscribe Button.description: A YouTube-style overlay in the lower-left corner with a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button, a bell icon, and the text 'CIARA O'DOHERTY Fashion & Style' next to a circular profile picture, appearing as Ciara encourages viewers to subscribe to her channel.

raw_segments: The video opens with a split-screen, showcasing a dynamic montage of street-style fashion on the left, featuring women in chic, monochromatic outfits like black pinstripe blazers, one-shoulder tops, white trousers, and sleek black suits, all carrying designer handbags and posing confidently in urban settings. Simultaneously, on the right, Ciara O'Doherty, wearing a blue denim shirt, large rectangular glasses, and gold hoop earrings, speaks expressively to the camera with hand gestures from a minimalist indoor space with white cabinetry and a beige vase. She discusses fashion trends in a clear, engaging voice, noting that "another element to this is peep toe shoes," acknowledging it "isn't for everyone" but is "definitely a trend that is coming back," and asks viewers where they "stand on the peep toe." Ciara continues, explaining that it's "very much in that vintage style" and that "when we talk about trends, it is all so cyclical," with "everything has its moment, everything comes back around eventually." The left side then transitions to a full-screen view of Ciara, who continues her commentary, summarizing "an overview of some of the key color trends that we're going to be seeing for 2025, as well as the main trend kind of aesthetics that we are going to be seeing for spring summer." She engages the audience further, asking what they think of these trends, what they love or hate, and what they are wearing or hesitant about. As she encourages viewers to "give me a thumbs up to let me know" if they liked the video and want more trend content, a YouTube-style 'SUBSCRIBE' button overlay appears in the lower-left corner, displaying her name 'CIARA O'DOHERTY' and the tagline 'Fashion & Style' alongside a circular profile picture and a bell icon. Ciara then explicitly asks viewers to "be sure to subscribe to my channel," mentioning she posts new videos weekly and would "love to have you back for more," before thanking them for watching. The bright, even lighting maintains a clean, professional aesthetic throughout.

event_blocks.summary: An upbeat, modern electronic-pop music track commences as a woman bids farewell, stating, "I'll see you on my next one. Bye." The visual montage follows the woman through various urban and park settings, showcasing different outfits. Initially, she is seen in a beige blazer over a black dress, carrying a Black Handbag, walking past a stone wall and black metal fence. The scene shifts to her in a black coat with a white top, holding a red coffee cup and a Brown Suede Handbag, near a modern building with a glass facade. Subsequently, she appears in a grey blazer over a black mini dress, carrying a Black Handbag, passing a classic black door with a wrought iron fence. The setting then transitions to a park with fallen autumn leaves, where she is observed in a beige trench coat over a ribbed dress, and later in a grey coat over a black dress, both times carrying a Black Handbag. The concluding scenes depict her in a beige trench coat with a black beret, walking past a stone building with large windows. A persistent end screen overlay displays 'Thanks For Watching', a Subscribe Button with a bell icon and the channel name 'CIARA O'DOHERTY Fashion & Style', and 'MORE TO WATCH' with empty video thumbnails.
